competitors.name: KnowBe4
competitors.entry_point: Phishing > Campaigns > +Create Phishing Campaign (email). Callback Phishing is a separate navigation area. No SMS campaign type exists.
competitors.setup_friction: Minimal for email — single-page form, no prerequisites. Callback Phishing requires Diamond tier. SMS simulation does not exist as a native feature.
core_flow: Select campaign name
Choose audience: All Users or Specific Groups
Filter and select template by topic and difficulty
Set frequency (one-time, bi-weekly, monthly)
Set send timing window and business hours preference
Set start date, time, and timezone
Set tracking duration
Launch
feedback_states: Campaign dashboard: Phish-prone Percentage, click count by day, bounced count
Bounced count with CSV download for failure diagnosis
competitors.error_prevention: None documented for standard campaigns. No pre-launch validation gates. Bounce handling is post-hoc (CSV download).
competitors.trust_model: No pre-launch compliance acknowledgment. No admin confirmation of impact before launch. Trust is implicit — admins are expected to configure correctly.
competitors.rbac_notes: Full admin or nothing below Platinum. Granular security roles (view-only campaign results without launch permission) are Platinum/Diamond only.
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Single-page form allows fast repeat creation. Smart Groups (Platinum/Diamond) enable behavioral auto-enrollment for follow-up campaigns.
competitors.ai_personalization: AI PhishFlip converts real-world phishing emails to simulation templates. No per-user AI targeting in standard phishing.
competitors.risk_score_surface: Phish-prone Percentage (PPP) is the primary risk metric — click rate, not a behavioral risk score.
competitors.remediation_path: Automated training enrollment on click/failure is configurable. Training assigned from the ModStore catalog.
competitors.vishing_notes: Direct outbound vishing (Vishing Security Tests) discontinued January 1, 2023 due to carrier compliance risk. Replacement: Callback Phishing (Diamond only) — email-initiated, employee calls inbound IVR. Not true adversarial vishing.
competitors.smishing_notes: No native SMS simulation feature. Not listed in Types of Simulated Phishing Tests. Blog covers smishing as a threat topic but this does not correspond to a product capability.
competitors.multi_channel_model: Implicit per-campaign-type. Each channel (email, callback, QR, USB) is a separate nav area. No unified multi-channel campaign. No per-user or per-group channel assignment.
competitors.source_notes: All findings from official KnowBe4 support documentation, changelogs, and KB articles. Vishing discontinuation confirmed via official KB article with specific January 2023 date.

competitors.name: Hoxhunt
competitors.entry_point: Two modes: Adaptive (program config, AI runs continuously) and Manual (per-campaign wizard). Channel enrollment is part of program setup, not per-campaign selection.
competitors.setup_friction: High initial compliance friction for SMS: requires signed legal amendment, documented opt-in proof (US/Canada), and works council documentation (EU). Enforced out-of-band. Once cleared, adaptive campaigns run without per-campaign admin input.
core_flow: Configure program: define risk cohorts by role, department, location
Enroll channels: email, SMS, Teams (each requires prerequisites)
Set frequency tiers by cohort (high-risk: weekly; baseline: monthly; new hire: 30/60/90-day track)
AI runs simulations continuously within configured parameters
Manual mode: select template, target group, frequency, launch
feedback_states: Unified Human Risk Dashboard: reporting rate, fail rate, time-to-report across all channels
SMS reporting integrates with native iOS Report Message functionality
Post-click micro-training fires immediately on click (30-60 second push notification)
competitors.error_prevention: Carrier-aware geographic distribution for SMS (suggests routing logic exists). Pre-enrollment compliance gate prevents launch until consent documentation is confirmed out-of-band.
competitors.trust_model: Compliance requirements (legal amendment, opt-in proof, works council docs) communicated as prerequisites before SMS enrollment. Trust model is front-loaded into the onboarding process, not the per-campaign wizard.
competitors.rbac_notes: Enterprise RBAC with delegated admin, multi-entity (region/BU) structures, and audit logs. Key split: who can modify program parameters (cohort rules, channel enrollment, frequency) vs. who can view the Human Risk Dashboard.
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Adaptive model removes per-campaign decisions entirely after initial setup. Program runs and adjusts automatically. Manual mode supports repeat campaign creation with template library.
competitors.ai_personalization: Adaptive targeting driven by AI inference — cohort rules + behavioral history drive simulation frequency and channel selection per user. Most sophisticated targeting model reviewed.
competitors.risk_score_surface: Human Risk Dashboard tracks behavioral trend over time across channels. Fail rate, reporting rate, and time-to-report are the primary signals — not a single composite risk score.
competitors.remediation_path: Post-click micro-training (30-60 second push notification) fires immediately on click. Full training module assignment is also available. Reporting integrates with the threat intelligence feed.
competitors.vishing_notes: Two modes: (a) Callback simulations — email-initiated inbound IVR, similar to KnowBe4 Callback Phishing. (b) AI deepfake voice/video targeting executive-support roles — custom managed engagement, not self-serve.
competitors.smishing_notes: Real SMS to devices confirmed. Phone number provisioning via SCIM or CSV import required. Carrier-aware geographic distribution documented. iOS Report Message integration confirmed.
competitors.multi_channel_model: Channel enrollment is at program level. Per-user cross-channel routing (user A gets SMS, user B gets Teams based on role) is inferred to be AI-driven, not explicit admin configuration.
competitors.source_notes: Product pages, help docs, and changelog for SMS and compliance framework. Deepfake vishing engagement model from product pages — terms less precise, labeled as inference.

competitors.name: Proofpoint SAT
competitors.entry_point: SMS/smishing listed as a named campaign type alongside email — channel selection is a top-level campaign-type decision. Lucy (acquired) adds WhatsApp support but in Expert Mode only.
competitors.setup_friction: Lucy SMS requires: Expert Mode (simplified wizard excluded), formal written consent document submitted to SMS carrier, 4-week carrier whitelisting lead time (range: 4 days to 4 weeks by jurisdiction), CSM or Solutions Engineer involvement recommended before first SMS campaign.
core_flow: Select campaign type (email, smishing, QR, USB)
Select or configure template
Define target audience by group/cohort
Set delivery schedule
Launch
feedback_states: Threat intelligence integration: real-world lure conversion via AI ThreatFlip (email only)
Auto-enrollment of high-risk users into training documented
competitors.error_prevention: No in-product pre-launch compliance gate documented. Lucy's 4-week whitelisting is framed as failure prevention — a prerequisite step, not an in-wizard guardrail.
competitors.trust_model: No in-product compliance acknowledgment. Carrier whitelisting and consent are handled out-of-band at the MSA/contract layer. No admin-facing confirmation of impact before launch.
competitors.rbac_notes: Not detailed in reviewed public sources.
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Satori AI provides threat-informed simulation recommendations by group/cohort, reducing manual template selection for repeat campaigns.
competitors.ai_personalization: AI ThreatFlip converts real-world phishing emails to simulation templates in one click — email only. Satori AI recommends simulations by cohort.
competitors.remediation_path: Auto-enrollment of high-risk users into training is documented.
competitors.vishing_notes: Not confirmed in Proofpoint's main product documentation.
competitors.smishing_notes: Confirmed as first-class campaign type with thousands of templates based on real-world lures. Lucy adds WhatsApp (v5.5) but Expert Mode only.
competitors.multi_channel_model: Parallel single-channel campaigns. Picking smishing templates is a top-level campaign-type choice. No unified multi-channel orchestration documented.
competitors.source_notes: Main product page confirms SMS as campaign type but depth is unclear from public docs. LUCY integration status inferred — WhatsApp support is from Lucy's own pre-acquisition changelog, not confirmed in Proofpoint's current product marketing.

competitors.name: Cofense
competitors.entry_point: PhishMe Playbooks — a guided wizard configuring a 12-month email simulation program. Azure AD sync provisions targets automatically. Non-email channels have no self-serve wizard entry point.
competitors.setup_friction: Email: low (Playbook wizard, AD sync). SMS/WhatsApp: zero real-device sends — in-platform LMS simulation only. Vishing: full managed-service engagement (scope call, target list, script review, scheduling).
core_flow: Email: Configure Playbook (12-month program in a few clicks)
SMS/WhatsApp: Create LMS module showing mock SMS/WhatsApp interface
Vishing: Submit engagement request to Cofense services team; joint scope call; target list submission; script review; Cofense callers execute
feedback_states: Email: Campaign dashboard in admin console
Vishing: Post-engagement report delivered as PDF/presentation by Cofense services team — not in dashboard
Reporter button: Flagged emails feed into Cofense Triage for SOC analysis
competitors.error_prevention: Explicit policy decision: avoids real-device SMS simulation to prevent TCPA/GDPR/PECR exposure. Compliance for vishing handled by Cofense services team during intake.
competitors.trust_model: Cofense publishes an explicit blog post warning customers against real-device SMS simulation ('Thinking of Smishing Your Employees? Think Twice') citing TCPA, GDPR, and UK PECR. Trust model: Cofense takes liability off the customer by not sending real SMS.
competitors.rbac_notes: Access controls and AD group sync documented. Vishing RBAC: customer admin initiates and views, Cofense services team executes. No 'launch voice campaign' button for admins.
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Playbooks enable multi-scenario programs configured once. Reporter button creates ongoing feedback loop for email. No efficiency mechanisms documented for non-email channels.
competitors.ai_personalization: Not prominently featured in reviewed sources.
competitors.risk_score_surface: Not prominently featured in reviewed sources.
competitors.remediation_path: Reporter button integration with Cofense Triage for SOC response. Training assignment via the LMS after simulation failure.
competitors.vishing_notes: Fully managed service launched December 2023. Trained Cofense callers with IVR scripts. Employees are informed the call is part of sanctioned training — not fully deceptive. Not self-serve.
competitors.smishing_notes: In-platform LMS simulation only. No real SMS to employee devices. Explicitly avoids real-device SMS due to TCPA/GDPR/UK PECR exposure.
competitors.multi_channel_model: Multi-channel is marketed but each channel uses a different delivery model: email (self-serve), SMS/WhatsApp (LMS module, no real send), vishing (managed service). No unified campaign orchestration.
competitors.source_notes: In-platform SMS simulation stance from official Cofense blog post with explicit TCPA/GDPR reasoning. Vishing as managed service from product pages and December 2023 press release.

competitors.name: Gophish
competitors.entry_point: Direct campaign creation from dashboard. 3-step flow: name + template + targets + launch.
competitors.setup_friction: None. Open source, no prerequisites, no compliance gates.
core_flow: Name the campaign
Select or create an HTML email template
Select a target group (CSV import or manual)
Set SMTP sending profile
Launch
feedback_states: Real-time dashboard: opens, clicks, credential submissions
competitors.error_prevention: None. No validation gates, no compliance acknowledgment, no coverage preview.
competitors.trust_model: None — open tool, operator is fully responsible.
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Template library enables fast reuse. Campaign clone is standard.
competitors.vishing_notes: Not supported. Red teamers extend Gophish with external SMS tooling (Twilio, custom scripts).
competitors.smishing_notes: Not supported natively. The gap between Gophish's clean UX and the operational complexity of real-device SMS is the design opportunity.
competitors.source_notes: Open source — fully observable. All findings are direct observations.

observed_patterns: Channel selection is a campaign-type decision (top-level), not a wizard-step toggle — seen in KnowBe4, Proofpoint, Lucy
Per-campaign-type separate navigation areas prevent multi-channel single-campaign orchestration — all reviewed platforms
Behavioral cohort targeting with AI-driven frequency tiers — Hoxhunt
Unified behavioral dashboard across channels with consistent metrics — Hoxhunt
Post-click micro-training firing immediately on click (30-60 second push) — Hoxhunt
Smart Groups / dynamic behavioral groups for auto-targeting repeat failures — KnowBe4 Platinum/Diamond
In-platform LMS simulation as an alternative to real-device SMS sends — Cofense
Playbook model (configure 12-month program once) for email simulation — Cofense
Carrier whitelisting as a prerequisite step handled out-of-band before SMS launch — Lucy
Vishing as managed professional service with intake process — Cofense, Hoxhunt deepfake mode
anti_patterns: Smishing locked to Expert Mode / power-user flow — prevents simplified wizard from handling SMS compliance guardrails (Lucy)
Compliance gates handled out-of-band with no in-product status visibility — all reviewed platforms
No per-user delivery failure surface or fallback routing — all reviewed platforms
Post-campaign failure diagnosis via CSV download only — KnowBe4
Multi-channel reporting siloed by channel (email in dashboard, vishing as PDF deliverable) — Cofense
Vishing discontinued due to carrier compliance risk without a clear self-serve successor — KnowBe4
Real-device SMS avoided via in-platform LMS simulation — reduces adversarial realism to near zero (Cofense)
No pre-launch compliance acknowledgment for email campaigns — all reviewed platforms except Hoxhunt (out-of-band)
differentiation_opportunities: Unified multi-channel campaign orchestration — single campaign that routes different users to different channels based on role, device profile, or risk score; no platform currently supports this

Embedded pre-flight compliance checklist — carrier whitelist status, consent documentation state, and works council clearance surfaced inline in the campaign review step; currently handled out-of-band by all competitors

Per-user delivery failure surface with fallback routing — channel-specific failure reasons and an explicit fallback decision (retry on alternate channel, exclude, notify admin); absent across all reviewed platforms
Self-serve vishing with carrier-compliant architecture — KnowBe4 discontinued, Cofense and Hoxhunt moved to managed service; the gap is real but requires solving carrier compliance first
Behavioral risk score as a targeting input for red team campaigns — target users above a risk score threshold rather than broad group targeting; uses Dune's existing risk model as a campaign input
Unified behavioral reporting across all channels in one view — Hoxhunt does this best but Dune's risk score integration with training and remediation creates a richer signal set
design_implications: Channel selection should be a top-level campaign-type decision before entering the wizard — pre-filters templates, compliance steps, and targeting options to what's relevant for that channel
Coverage indicator must be per-channel (X have SMS, Y have WhatsApp, Z have both, W have neither) — a single aggregate percentage is insufficient for multi-channel targeting

Design a compliance pre-flight panel as a first-class wizard step — surface carrier whitelist status, consent documentation state, and any works council flags inline with a 'not ready to launch' state if unresolved

The vishing architecture question must be resolved before any vishing UI is designed — KnowBe4's discontinuation demonstrates that self-serve outbound vishing has carrier compliance exposure that has forced a major vendor to shut the feature down

Design per-user delivery failure states explicitly in the campaign detail view — channel-specific failure reasons (carrier rejection, invalid number, not on WhatsApp) with fallback action available at the row level

Do not neuter real-device delivery to simplify compliance — Cofense's in-platform SMS simulation is not red teaming; the compliance model must be rigorous, but messages must actually reach employee devices
confidence_notes: KnowBe4 vishing discontinuation confirmed via official KB article with January 2023 date — high confidence

Hoxhunt SMS compliance requirements (legal amendment, opt-in proof, works council docs) confirmed from product pages and help documentation — high confidence; enforcement mechanism (out-of-band) is an inference
Proofpoint WhatsApp support inferred from Lucy v5.5 changelog pre-acquisition — not confirmed in current Proofpoint product marketing; treat as medium confidence
Cofense in-platform SMS simulation stance confirmed from official blog post with explicit TCPA/GDPR reasoning — high confidence
Lucy 4-week carrier whitelisting and Expert Mode constraint confirmed from official Lucy wiki documentation — high confidence
Gophish findings are direct observations from open source codebase and product site — high confidence
